title: Interval Timing Task
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 139 participants (94 with Parkinson's disease and 45 healthy controls) performing an interval timing task. Participants judged the elapsed time of either 3-second or 7-second intervals while 64-channel EEG was recorded. The study investigates temporal perception and motor response timing in the context of neurodegenerative disease, with potential implications for understanding basal ganglia function in time estimation.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a 64-channel BrainVision cap during an interval timing task. Participants viewed instructional cues ('Short interval' or 'Long interval') for 1 second, followed by a visual cue (solid box) marking interval onset. Intervals lasted 6 seconds (3-second trials) or 14 seconds (7-second trials), with distractors presented at random intervals. Participants responded via spacebar when they judged the target interval to have elapsed.
